info.name: Qeet Id - API
info.description:
Complete request collection for the qeet-id backend (Go monolith).

## Setup

1. Start the backend: `cd backend && make run` (defaults to `http://localhost:4000`).
2. Import this collection AND the companion environment (`qeet-id.postman_environment.json`).
3. Select the **qeet-id (local)** environment in the Postman environment dropdown.
4. **First time / fresh DB:** run **Auth → Signup**. It creates a new tenant + user + owner role in one shot and auto-populates `{{tenantId}}`, `{{userId}}`, `{{accessToken}}`, `{{refreshToken}}`. The signup user becomes the tenant admin.
5. **Returning users:** run **Auth → Login** with the saved tenant_id + email + password.

## Conventions

- All authed requests inherit a `Bearer {{accessToken}}` header from the collection root.
- IDs (`{{tenantId}}`, `{{userId}}`, `{{roleId}}`, …) are written back to collection variables by the corresponding `Create` / `Signup` requests, so you can chain them.
- Routes prefixed `/v1/...` are versioned API surface. Discovery / JWKS live at root (`/.well-known/...`) per the OIDC spec.

exec: throw new Error('baseUrl is not set. Select the qeet-id (local) environment or override --env-var baseUrl=...');
// The backend CSRF middleware (httpx/csrf.go) requires non-Bearer mutation
// requests to carry (a) an allow-listed Origin/Referer and (b) a double-
// submit cookie pair (qe_csrf + X-CSRF-Token). Bearer-token traffic skips
// entirely. Postman is not a browser so it sends neither header — handle
// it transparently here so the public auth routes (signup/login/recovery/
// invite-accept/oauth-token) work out of the box.
// If we already have a cached CSRF token from a prior seed, just echo it.
// Otherwise, seed the cookie by GETting /healthz and extract qe_csrf
// from Set-Cookie. Postman will wait for this async call before firing
pm.sendRequest({ url: baseUrl + '/healthz', method: 'GET' }, function (err, res) {
// Skip everything if the request errored out (no response, e.g. connection refused).
// Universal sanity checks. Per-request tests still run.
// Safety-net token capture: if any response carries access_token / refresh_token,
// keep collection vars in sync. Per-request tests still own the primary capture.
} catch (e) { /* non-JSON or empty body — ignore */ }

request.description: Prometheus exposition format. Public, root-level (no /v1).

exec: console.log('Signed up — you are now owner of tenant', body.tenant_id);
// Persist the email/password actually sent so Login can re-use them.

request.description:
Public self-service signup. Takes only `{email, password, display_name?}`. In ONE transaction the backend:

1. Generates a personal tenant for the new user (slug like `personal-<12hex>`, name like `"Jane's workspace"`).
2. Creates the user with a password credential.
3. Creates a system `owner` role for the personal tenant and grants it every platform permission.
4. Assigns the user to that owner role.
5. Issues an access + refresh token pair (auto-login).

The user can rename their workspace or create more from `Tenants → New tenant`.

**Email is globally unique** — same address can't sign up twice across the platform.

**Errors:**
- 409 — email already in use
- 422 — validation failed (password < 8 chars, invalid email, …)
Hosted-login end-user self-registration into an existing tenant (B2C).
Hosted-login: verifies credentials and sets the HttpOnly SSO cookie (qe_ls). Postman stores the cookie in its jar for subsequent /v1/oauth/authorize calls.
Hosted-login: finish a two-step login and set the SSO cookie.
Hosted-login logout: revoke the SSO session and clear the qe_ls cookie.
Admin clears a user's MFA (TOTP/recovery/OTP). Requires user.write; audited as mfa.admin_reset.

item.name: Authed call with API key — GET /v1/users (ApiKey auth)

request.description: Exchange a social provider's authorization code for a qeet-id token pair.

exec: if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length && arr[0].id !== undefined) pm.collectionVariables.set('deliveryId', arr[0].id);

request.description: Verifies the tamper-evident hash chain across audit events for the tenant. Returns ok=true plus the verified range; ok=false with break_at when a tampered row is detected.
Returns rollup metrics for the admin dashboard: total users, MAU, signups vs prior period, signins, failure rates, weekly activity (last 8 weeks).
Inspects dead-lettered outbox events for ops triage. Requires platform-admin gating in the deployment.

exec: // chain: remember the created SCIM user id
try { const b = pm.response.json(); if (b.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('scimUserId', b.id); } catch (e) {}
try { const r = pm.response.json(); const a = r.Resources || []; if (a.length) pm.collectionVariables.set('scimUserId', a[0].id); } catch (e) {}
try { const r = pm.response.json(); const a = r.Resources || []; if (a.length) pm.collectionVariables.set('scimGroupId', a[0].id); } catch (e) {}

request.description: Preflight-validate an inbound SAML IdP connection before rollout.
Register a downstream service provider that trusts Qeet as its SAML IdP.
SP-side ACS (CSRF-exempt, signature-validated). The IdP POSTs the assertion here.
Qeet-as-IdP metadata XML (public).

body.raw:
{
  "password_enabled": true,
  "password_min_length": 12,
  "password_require_uppercase": true,
  "password_require_number": true,
  "password_require_symbol": false,
  "magic_link_enabled": true,
  "magic_link_ttl_minutes": 30,
  "passkey_enabled": true,
  "otp_email_enabled": false,
  "otp_sms_enabled": false
}

request.description: Token vaulting: fetch a secret by name with a scoped principal (e.g. an AI agent). Requires scope vault:<name> or vault:read — uses {{agentAccessToken}}. Set {{secretName}} to a secret created in this folder.
Begin a paid plan change via hosted Stripe/Razorpay checkout. Requires the provider env keys to be configured server-side.
Provider webhook (path is the provider: stripe | razorpay). Signature-verified server-side — Postman calls will be rejected without a valid provider signature.
Hosted-login UI context for an in-flight authorize request (client name, providers).
Record the user's consent decision; returns the authorization code redirect.
RFC 8628 §3.1 device flow initiation.
Root-level (no /v1). Client-authenticated. Always returns 200 per the RFC.
Root-level (no /v1). Client-authenticated. Returns {active, scope, actor_type, ...}.
Issuer signing-key metadata (no private key material).

RFC 8693 token exchange via the shared /v1/oauth/token-code endpoint. Downscope-only (never escalates). Drop actor_token for plain downscoping; include it for delegation (records an `act` claim). Requires the client to be permitted the token-exchange grant.
RFC 8628 §3.4 device-code polling against the shared /v1/oauth/token-code endpoint. Poll after Device authorization + Device decision (approve).

exec: const r = pm.response.json(); const a = r.items || [];

request.description: Register a confidential OIDC client under the tenant. Returns the row id (used by PATCH/DELETE/rotate) plus the public client_id and one-time client_secret.

exec: const b = pm.response.json(); const cl = b.client || b;

request.description: Tenant-scoped by the caller's token; takes the client row id.
Fine-grained authz tuples on an object. The 'object' query param is required.
subject may be 'user:<id>' or a userset like 'team:eng#member'.
Recursive userset check (with cycle guard). Returns {allowed: bool}.

exec: if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length && arr[0].id !== undefined) pm.collectionVariables.set('anomalyId', arr[0].id);
if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length && arr[0].id !== undefined) pm.collectionVariables.set('agentId', arr[0].id);

request.description: Registers an AI-agent identity. The plaintext secret is returned ONCE on create.
Exchange agent credentials for a short-lived, downscoped actor_type=agent token. Use {{agentAccessToken}} for the Vault read.
Issue a W3C JWT-VC. Returns {id, credential} — credential is the signed JWT-VC.
Public: any relying party can verify a presented JWT-VC. Returns validity + revocation.

exec: if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length && arr[0].id !== undefined) pm.collectionVariables.set('authHookId', arr[0].id);

request.description: Synchronous post-credential allow/deny gate. fail_open=true keeps logins working if the hook endpoint is unreachable.

exec: if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length && arr[0].id !== undefined) pm.collectionVariables.set('domainId', arr[0].id);

request.description: Returns the DNS TXT record (name/type/value) to publish for verification.
Performs the DNS TXT lookup and marks the domain verified if it matches.

exec: if (Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length && arr[0].id !== undefined) pm.collectionVariables.set('logSinkId', arr[0].id);

request.description: Stream audit/security events to a SIEM. type ∈ {splunk_hec, datadog, http}.
Current principal's in-app inbox + unread count.
